name: Trip planning
goal: Lock when and where you're going, book the big pieces, and leave with a trip you can actually enjoy.
description: Spread over about four weeks so you're not doing everything the night before: pick dates and place, book how you'll get there, book where you'll sleep, then sketch days so you have room to wander. Dates shift to start from the day you create this plan.

tasks.title: Pick your dates and destination
tasks.content:
Nail down the window you're traveling and where you're headed (even if it's "city A with a day trip to B").

Compare a few options:
- Price and weather for 2-3 date ranges if you can flex
- Any hard constraints: PTO, school breaks, weddings, work peaks

Don't forget the boring stuff:
- Passports valid long enough? Visas or entry rules?
- Rough budget so the next steps don't give you sticker shock

tasks.title: Book flights, trains, or your road trip
tasks.content:
Lock in how you're getting there and back (or your rental car if you're driving).

Tips:
- If dates might move, favor flexible fares or note change fees before you pay
- Set a fare alert if you're still watching prices

After you book:
- Stash confirmation numbers in one place (notes app, email folder, or trip doc)
- Add departures and check-in windows to your calendar so they don't sneak up on you

tasks.title: Reserve where you'll stay
tasks.content:
Choose lodging that matches how you'll move: walkable neighborhood, near transit, or next to the one thing you care about most.

Before you commit:
- Skim recent reviews for noise, safety, Wi-Fi, and accuracy of photos
- Check check-in and check-out times and whether you can store bags

If the rate is non-refundable, have a backup option in mind so you're not stuck if plans change

tasks.title: Sketch your days (and leave breathing room)
tasks.content:
You don't need a minute-by-minute spreadsheet. You need must-sees, a few reservations, and space to rest.

Worth booking early:
- Popular museums, tours, or restaurants that sell out

Share the plan:
- Send a simple outline to anyone you're traveling with

Offline safety net:
- Save offline maps and jot down emergency numbers and your hotel address in the local language if it helps
